manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: jig, peck, plump, toast, pry, seal, hitch, truck, drill.
Your opponent's team words are: stripe, pool, rubber, pad, clutch, stable, mint, lean.
Distractor words are: corn, boil, bridge, strain, sap, rail, jumper.
The kill words are: file.

message.content:
text.CLUE: mechanical
TARGETS: jig, drill, hitch, truck, seal, pry
message.reasoning:
We need to give a one-word clue and list of target words from our team that it relates to. Must not be any word in any list or contain parts of them. Must not be a kill word. The clue must be semantically related to the target words.

Our team words: jig, peck, plump, toast, pry? Wait "pry" is a verb, also a tool maybe. seal, hitch, truck, drill.

We can try to relate multiple words with a single clue.

We could think "construction" relates to drill, truck, hitch (like hitch a trailer), seal (seal something), plump? not. "Construction" not in any list. But might also relate to "pry". Might be okay. However "construction" could also relate to "bridge" which is a distractor, but that's okay; clue can be related to distractors but risk.

Better maybe "mechanical" relates to drill, truck, hitch, seal (seal as mechanical seal), pry (pry bar). Could also relate to "jig" (a jig is a tool). So "mechanical" relates to jig, drill, truck, hitch, pry, seal. That's many. But "mechanical" is not a word in any list. Should be okay. Provide clue and list which we target: maybe "jig, drill, hitch, truck, seal, pry". However we must be careful: "mechanical" might also relate to opponent's "rubber" (mechanical rubber?), "stable"? maybe not. But risk is okay.

We must ensure clue is a single word, no spaces. maybe "mechanical". Provide list separated by commas.

Thus answer:

CLUE: mechanical
TARGETS: jig, drill, hitch, truck, seal, pry

Check: Are any of those words parts of clue? "mechanical" contains "seal"? No. Good.

Make sure not to include any other
